e6f9dc3b346d.title: AI Judge incorrectly flags 'passed-empty' and '0 cards produced' as functional bugs in cold-run tests
e6f9dc3b346d.expected: AI judge should interpret 'passed-empty' and '0 cards produced' as expected behavior (informational/non-issue) under cold-run conditions as defined by test context.
e6f9dc3b346d.actual: AI judge flags 'passed-empty' and '0 cards produced' as high-priority functional bugs, leading to over-flagging and wasted engineering effort.
e6f9dc3b346d.evidence:
Surfaced by the LLM Council peer-review (a blind spot the single judges missed):
Chairman verdict explicitly states these are 'expected artifacts of this setup, not product bugs' and that 'The AI judge's flags are over-flagged noise and constitute a bug in the automated testing framework itself.'

e883e6f0bbc7.title: Upload and processing completed successfully but yielded no content cards.
e883e6f0bbc7.expected: A successful upload and processing of a meet file (ARENA Manchester International) should generate content cards, especially when the flow result is 'passed-empty', indicating a successful run.
e883e6f0bbc7.actual: Despite a 'passed-empty' flow result and successful navigation through the application, zero content cards were generated for the 'ARENA Manchester International' meet. The trust summary explicitly states 'No content cards were generated.'
e883e6f0bbc7.evidence:
AI judge (functional, confidence high):
flow_result: passed-empty, content_summary: cards=0; trust={'overall_summary': 'No content cards were generated.'}
repro: Reproduce the flow (result: passed-empty) and inspect the functional aspect described above

71e02f586c45.title: Implement clear, immediate, and actionable user feedback when no content cards are generated due to insufficient data.
71e02f586c45.expected: When no content cards are generated due to insufficient data, the system should provide explicit feedback to the user explaining why content was not produced and offer next steps or alternatives.
71e02f586c45.actual: The system currently provides a cryptic 'passed-empty' status, which does not explain the lack of content or guide the user on how to proceed.
71e02f586c45.evidence:
Surfaced by the LLM Council peer-review (a blind spot the single judges missed):
Chairman verdict: 'The single most important fix is to implement clear, immediate, and actionable user feedback when no content cards are generated due to insufficient data.'

5cebfcb079da.title: Review and potentially broaden the definition and generation criteria for 'content cards' to provide value in data-sparse scenarios.
5cebfcb079da.expected: The system should either generate default or generic content cards even in the absence of specific data like Personal Bests (PBs), or redefine expectations around what constitutes 'content cards' to better serve user needs in various scenarios.
5cebfcb079da.actual: The current system's reliance on specific data (e.g., PBs) for content card generation leads to zero output in data-sparse scenarios, which is problematic from a user's perspective.
5cebfcb079da.evidence:
Surfaced by the LLM Council peer-review (a blind spot the single judges missed):
Blind Spot: The Council largely missed questioning the definition of 'content cards' itself. If the system is designed to produce cards based on PBs, then the absence of PBs logically leads to zero cards. This could imply a need to broaden the types of content cards generated or redefine expectations.

55093f7335f8.title: Investigate and refine the AI judge's logic for distinguishing 'functional problems' from 'expected artifacts' to prevent future false posit
55093f7335f8.expected: The AI judge should accurately reflect the user-facing impact of an outcome, even if it is technically an 'expected artifact' of the test context, to avoid flagging user-impacting issues as merely 'passed-empty' with high confidence.
55093f7335f8.actual: The AI judge flagged an 'expected outcome' ('passed-empty', cards=0) with 'high confidence' despite it representing a significant user problem, suggesting a potential flaw in its understanding of what constitutes a 'functional problem' versus an 'expected artifact'.
55093f7335f8.evidence:
Surfaced by the LLM Council peer-review (a blind spot the single judges missed):
Blind Spot: The Council overlooked the implications of the AI judge flagging an 'expected outcome' with 'high confidence.' This suggests a potential flaw in the AI judge's own understanding of what constitutes a 'functional problem' versus an 'expected artifact,' which could lead to future false positives.

510d8919d03e.title: Review page is blank after a successful run — dead end after upload
510d8919d03e.expected: After uploading a results file and getting 6 runs judged, the volunteer should land on a screen showing draft cards to approve, edit, or export
510d8919d03e.actual: review_text is entirely empty — no cards, no approve buttons, no export options, nothing
510d8919d03e.evidence:
AI judge (user_brain, confidence high):
flow_result confirms processing completed ('live:judged-6-runs') but review_text contains zero content; the core task (approve and get content) is completely unreachable
repro: Reproduce the flow (result: live:judged-6-runs) and inspect the user_brain aspect described above

b07572c63c13.title: Flow result exposes raw internal status string instead of a user message
b07572c63c13.expected: A plain-English confirmation like '6 moments found — ready to review' with a clear next-step button
b07572c63c13.actual: The status string 'live:judged-6-runs' is surfaced as the outcome — technical slug, no explanation, no CTA
b07572c63c13.evidence:
AI judge (user_brain, confidence high):
flow_result value is 'live:judged-6-runs'; combined with an empty review page this leaves the volunteer with no idea what happened or what to do next
repro: Reproduce the flow (result: live:judged-6-runs) and inspect the user_brain aspect described above

3f77367bbf6c.title: Two primary CTAs with different labels that appear to do the same thing
3f77367bbf6c.expected: One clear, consistent call-to-action to start creating content
3f77367bbf6c.actual: 'Create new content →' appears mid-page under the org status block; 'Start a content pack →' appears at the bottom of the page — different labels, no explanation of any difference
3f77367bbf6c.evidence:
AI judge (user_brain, confidence high):
Both appear on the home page with no distinction in surrounding copy; a volunteer cannot tell if these go to different flows or are the same action
repro: Reproduce the flow (result: live:judged-6-runs) and inspect the user_brain aspect described above

ec0d0cf5d7ca.title: 'Ready TO FILE' org status label is meaningless to a social-media volunteer
ec0d0cf5d7ca.expected: A status phrase that confirms the org is set up and ready to produce posts, e.g. 'Brand loaded — ready to create'
ec0d0cf5d7ca.actual: 'Ready TO FILE' — 'file' implies submitting documents or paperwork, not social media content
ec0d0cf5d7ca.evidence:
AI judge (user_brain, confidence high):
Exact text on home page: 'SWANSEA UNIVERSITY SWIMMING TEAM. Ready TO FILE.' — no explanation of what 'file' means in this context
repro: Reproduce the flow (result: live:judged-6-runs) and inspect the user_brain aspect described above

8ee1cbc395d9.title: Response body of /review/{id} routes never verified — 200 status does not confirm card content
8ee1cbc395d9.expected: A /review/{id} route returning HTTP 200 should have a non-empty body containing card data
8ee1cbc395d9.actual: Only status codes were checked; no advisor fetched a body — the 'cards exist because routes respond' thesis is entirely untested
8ee1cbc395d9.evidence:
Surfaced by the LLM Council peer-review (a blind spot the single judges missed):
Chairman: 'A route can return 200 with an empty or skeleton body. If those 13 routes return 200 with blank bodies, the contradiction dissolves.' This single check forks the entire repair strategy between render-path bug and generation-pipeline bug.

f07b0a820cda.title: Export routes unreachable — potential silent 404s independent of review-page block
f07b0a820cda.expected: Export endpoints for a processed run should return valid responses and be independently reachable at the route level
f07b0a820cda.actual: Export path was never checked; review page already blocks UI access, but route-level export endpoints may be 404ing silently as a second independent failure
f07b0a820cda.evidence:
Surfaced by the LLM Council peer-review (a blind spot the single judges missed):
Chairman: 'The context explicitly calls out a run whose export 404s as a genuine production bug. Nobody checked whether export routes are reachable. This is a silent second failure.'

790ea7991d21.evidence:
AI judge (user_brain, confidence medium):
'Your brand voice, palette, and logo are loaded. Captions, graphics, and motion videos will arrive on-brand.' — presupposes completed setup; no 'set up your brand' step or link visible anywhere on the page
repro: Reproduce the flow (result: live:judged-6-runs) and inspect the user_brain aspect described above

0787c98d002c.title: Card confidence filter may silently discard all generated cards with no observability
0787c98d002c.expected: Distinguishable signal between pipeline failure (cards never created) and threshold filtering (cards created then discarded), so the correct fix — pipeline repair vs threshold tuning — can be identified
0787c98d002c.actual: Only post-filter count is observable (cards=0); trust.overall_confidence='low' alongside cards=0 is ambiguous and does not confirm whether card objects were ever instantiated
0787c98d002c.evidence:
Surfaced by the LLM Council peer-review (a blind spot the single judges missed):
Chairman: 'Cards may have been generated and then filtered out below a confidence threshold. That is a different bug with a different fix — a tuning problem, not a pipeline crash. Pull the raw card objects before the confidence filter, not just the exported count.'

5fe1b78a67a5.title: Semantic subagents skipped — no AI provider configured

8c7e2f131136.title: No Clear Path to Content Creation for New Users

The 'Start a content pack →' button is also present, but its immediate destination and user experience for a new user are unknown.

AI judge (user_brain, confidence high):
The 'home_text' is for an existing user. The absence of a signup flow means the new user journey is untested.

review_text field is "" (empty string)

[council triage] demoted — Symptom of [0], not an independent defect; retest after [0] is resolved

644fc7499a4c.title: Flow result is raw developer status string, not a usable screen
644fc7499a4c.expected: A human-readable outcome like '6 posts are ready for your review' with a clear button to open the drafts
644fc7499a4c.actual: flow_result is "live:judged-6-runs" — opaque jargon with no call to action; a volunteer would not know what this means or what to click next
644fc7499a4c.evidence:
AI judge (user_brain, confidence high):
flow_result = "live:judged-6-runs"

[council triage] demoted — UX debt; opaque status string does not block a volunteer today, not a sprint ticket
repro: Reproduce the flow (result: live:judged-6-runs) and inspect the user_brain aspect described above

7a51dcd95351.title: No sign-in or sign-up entry point in navigation
7a51dcd95351.expected: An unauthenticated visitor sees a prominent SIGN IN or GET STARTED button to create an account
7a51dcd95351.actual: Navigation lists only: HOME, CREATE, ORGANISATION, MEDIA LIBRARY, SETTINGS, SWITCH ORG, SIGN OUT — no path to sign up or log in for a new visitor
7a51dcd95351.evidence:
AI judge (user_brain, confidence high):
Full nav text: "HOME / CREATE / ORGANISATION / MEDIA LIBRARY / SETTINGS / SWITCH ORG / SIGN OUT"

[council triage] demoted — False positive: SIGN OUT in nav confirms authenticated sweep; absent sign-in entry point is correct for a logged-in session
repro: Reproduce the flow (result: live:judged-6-runs) and inspect the user_brain aspect described above

2f70e2b4c101.title: No visible file upload control on the home page
2f70e2b4c101.expected: A drop zone, upload button, or clear link that lets the volunteer immediately upload their Hytek .hy3 file — the core task they came to do
2f70e2b4c101.actual: Home page has two vague CTAs ('Create new content →' and 'Start a content pack →') but no upload widget, no drag-and-drop zone, and no indication either button leads to file upload
2f70e2b4c101.evidence:
AI judge (user_brain, confidence high):
Workflow step 01 promises 'Upload a Hytek results file' but home_text contains no upload UI; CTAs label text gives no indication of destination

[council triage] demoted — UX debt; vague CTAs are an improvement item, not a blocker for the core task
repro: Reproduce the flow (result: live:judged-6-runs) and inspect the user_brain aspect described above

275f6d4f3c15.title: 'WE CRAWL Club site & socials' disclosed as a feature with no consent or opt-out shown
275f6d4f3c15.expected: If the tool scrapes the club's website and social accounts, the volunteer should be able to opt in/out during onboarding, with scope clearly explained
275f6d4f3c15.actual: Crawling is listed as a built-in capability alongside file reading, with no explanation of what is crawled, when, or how to limit it — a volunteer may not realise their club's accounts are being scraped
275f6d4f3c15.evidence:
AI judge (user_brain, confidence high):
"WE CRAWL / Club site & socials" in the home feature grid; no consent UI visible anywhere in the captured screens

[council triage] demoted — Policy and product decision; crawl disclosure is not a sprint blocker
repro: Reproduce the flow (result: live:judged-6-runs) and inspect the user_brain aspect described above

32d69a65464d.title: No sign-up or log-in CTA visible to a new visitor
32d69a65464d.expected: A first-time visitor should see a 'Sign up' or 'Get started' button to create an account
32d69a65464d.actual: Nav shows 'SWITCH ORG' and 'SIGN OUT' — authenticated-user controls only; no route in for someone who doesn't have an account yet
32d69a65464d.evidence:
AI judge (user_brain, confidence high):
Nav items: HOME / CREATE / ORGANISATION / MEDIA LIBRARY / SETTINGS / SWITCH ORG / SIGN OUT — every item assumes an existing session

[council triage] demoted — Observation gap — sweep ran authenticated; missing sign-up CTA is unconfirmed for unauthenticated visitors
repro: Reproduce the flow (result: live:judged-6-runs) and inspect the user_brain aspect described above

9ffb1407aa9a.title: Two ambiguous primary CTAs with no differentiation
9ffb1407aa9a.expected: One clear action like 'Upload results file' that matches what the volunteer came to do
9ffb1407aa9a.actual: 'Create new content →' (hero) and 'Start a content pack →' (footer) appear to do similar things; neither label says 'upload a file' or references Hytek/.hy3
9ffb1407aa9a.evidence:
AI judge (user_brain, confidence high):
Both buttons appear on the same home page with no explanation of how they differ; the workflow section says step 1 is 'Upload a Hytek results file' but neither CTA uses that language

[council triage] demoted — Copy feedback only, not a functional defect; defer
repro: Reproduce the flow (result: live:judged-6-runs) and inspect the user_brain aspect described above
